message:
docs(readme): fix select_dtypes(strs) → select_dtypes_by_name (br-rb1gy / fd90.170)

fd90.170: line 1530 in Selection and Indexing showed:

  let numeric = df.select_dtypes(&["int64", "float64"], &[])?;

But crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs has TWO variants:
  line 25920: pub fn select_dtypes(&self, include: &[DType], exclude: &[DType])
  line 25950: pub fn select_dtypes_by_name(&self, include: &[&str], exclude: &[&str])

The string-name variant is select_dtypes_by_name. The plain
select_dtypes takes &[DType] enum values.

Inconsistency: line 1119 in Advanced Selection Methods correctly uses:
  df.select_dtypes(&[DType::Int64, DType::Float64], &[])?
The Selection and Indexing snippet was likely written before the API
split between enum-typed and string-typed variants.

Fix: switch line 1530 to select_dtypes_by_name to match the &str
literals being shown. Inline note clarifies the enum-form alternative
for future readers.

Methods are reached via the in-prelude DataFrame type — no prelude
expansion needed.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
